item.title: Getting Started with Malware Analysis
item.author: dizonephraimjoseph@gmail.com (Ephraim Joseph Dizon)
item.description:
Introduction

Malware analysis is the process of understanding the behavior, purpose, and impact of a suspicious file or program. Whether you’re responding to an incident or proactively hunting threats, having a solid foundation in malware analysis is essential for any security analyst.

In this post, I’ll walk through how to get started — from setting up a safe lab environment to performing your first static and dynamic analysis.

Setting Up Your Lab

Before touching any malicious file, you need an isolated environment. Never analyze malware on your host machine.
